28	class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
29	    """
30	    ### LoRA weights
31	    G = G + Ag @ Bg
32	    U = U + Au @ Bu
33	    W = W + Aw @ Bw
34	
35	    ### SwiGLU(X)
36	    e = X @ G
37	    f = e * sigmoid(e)
38	    g = X @ U
39	    h = f * g
40	    i = h @ W
41	
42	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
43	    See our blog post for more details
44	
45	    df = sigmoid(e) * (1 - f) + f
46	    dC/dW = h.T @ dY
47	    dC/dU = X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
48	    dC/dG = X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
49	
50	    ### Down projection LoRA weights
51	    dC/dAw = dC/dW @ B.T
52	    dC/dBw = A.T @ dC/dW
53	    dC/dAw =       h.T @ dY @ B.T
54	    dC/dBw = A.T @ h.T @ dY
55	
56	    ### Up projection LoRA weights
57	    dC/dAu =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * f) @ B.T
58	    dC/dBu = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
59	
60	    ### Gate projection LoRA weights
61	    dC/dAg =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g) @ B.T
62	    dC/dBg = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
63	
64	    Don't forget to see our blog post for more details!
65	    """
66	    @staticmethod
67	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
68	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
69	                gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
70	                  upW,   upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
71	                downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
72	                _forward_function, _backward_function,
73	                inplace = True,):
74	        dtype = X.dtype
75	
76	        e = matmul_lora(X, gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS)
77	        g = matmul_lora(X,   upW,   upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS)
78	        h = _forward_function(e, g)
79	        i = matmul_lora(h, downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS)
80	
81	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
82	            gateW, gateW_quant, gateS,
83	            upW, upW_quant, upS,
84	            downW, downW_quant, downS,
85	            _backward_function,
86	        )
87	        ctx.save_for_backward(gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB,
88	                              X, e, g)
89	        ctx.inplace = inplace
90	        return i
91	    pass
92	
93	
94	    @staticmethod
95	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
96	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
97	        gateW, gateW_quant, gateS, upW, upW_quant, upS, downW, downW_quant, downS, \
98	            _backward_function = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
99	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB, \
100	            X, e, g = ctx.saved_tensors
101	
102	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
103	        dY = dY.view(-1, dY.shape[-1])
104	        X  = X .view(-1, X .shape[-1])
105	        e  = e .view(-1, e .shape[-1])
106	        g  = g .view(-1, g .shape[-1])
107	        dtype = X.dtype
108	
109	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
110	            gateA.to(dtype), gateB.to(dtype), upA.to(dtype), upB.to(dtype), downA.to(dtype), downB.to(dtype)
111	
112	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
113	            gateA.t(), gateB.t(), upA.t(), upB.t(), downA.t(), downB.t()
114	
115	        DW = matmul_lora(dY, downW.t(), downW_quant, downB, downA, downS)
116	        DW, e, g = _backward_function(DW, e, g)
117	        h, df, de = DW, e, g
118	
119	        d_downA = torch.empty_like(downA)
120	        d_downB = torch.empty_like(downB)
121	        d_gateA = torch.empty_like(gateA)
122	        d_gateB = torch.empty_like(gateB)
123	        d_upA   = torch.empty_like(upA)
124	        d_upB   = torch.empty_like(upB)
125	
126	        # Down projection LoRA weights
127	        # d_downA = h.t() @ (dY @ downB.t())
128	        # d_downB = (downA.t() @ h.t()) @ dY
129	        # d_downA *= downS
130	        # d_downB *= downS
131	        d_downA.addmm_(h.t(), dY @ downB.t(), alpha = downS, beta = 0)
132	        d_downB.addmm_(downA.t() @ h.t(), dY, alpha = downS, beta = 0)
133	
134	        # Up projection LoRA weights
135	        # d_upA   = X.t() @ (df @ upB.t())
136	        # d_upB   = (upA.t() @ X.t()) @ df
137	        # d_upA  *= upS
138	        # d_upB  *= upS
139	        d_upA.addmm_(X.t(), df @ upB.t(), alpha = upS, beta = 0)
140	        d_upB.addmm_(upA.t() @ X.t(), df, alpha = upS, beta = 0)
141	
142	        # Gate projection LoRA weights
143	        # d_gateA = X.t() @ (de @ gateB.t())
144	        # d_gateB = (gateA.t() @ X.t()) @ de
145	        # d_gateA *= gateS
146	        # d_gateB *= gateS
147	        d_gateA.addmm_(X.t(), de @ gateB.t(), alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
148	        d_gateB.addmm_(gateA.t() @ X.t(), de, alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
149	
150	        # dX  = matmul_lora(df, upW.t(), upW_quant, upB, upA, upS)
151	        # dX += matmul_lora(de, gateW.t(), gateW_quant, gateB, gateA, gateS)
152	        upW = fast_dequantize(upW.t(), upW_quant)
153	        dX = torch.matmul(df, upW.t(), out = X if ctx.inplace else None)
154	        del upW
155	        # dX += df @ upB.to(dtype).t() @ (upS * upA.to(dtype).t())
156	        dX.addmm_(df @ upB.t(), upA.t(), alpha = upS)
157	
158	        gateW = fast_dequantize(gateW.t(), gateW_quant)
159	        # dX += de @ gateW.t()
160	        dX.addmm_(de, gateW.t())
161	        del gateW
162	        # dX += de @ gateB.to(dtype).t() @ (gateS * gateA.to(dtype).t())
163	        dX.addmm_(de @ gateB.t(), gateA.t(), alpha = gateS)
164	
165	        # gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
166	        #  upW,    upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS,
167	        # downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
168	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
169	            None, None, d_gateA.t(), d_gateB.t(), None, \
170	            None, None,   d_upA.t(),   d_upB.t(), None, \
171	            None, None, d_downA.t(), d_downB.t(), None, \
172	            None, None, None, # _backward and _forward and inplace
173	    pass
174	pass

224	class LoRA_QKV(torch.autograd.Function):
225	    """
226	    ### LoRA weights
227	    Wq = Wq + Aq @ Bq
228	    Wk = Wk + Ak @ Bk
229	    Wv = Wv + Av @ Bv
230	    Q = X @ Wq = X @ Wq + X @ Aq @ Bq
231	    K = X @ Wk = X @ Wk + X @ Ak @ Bk
232	    V = X @ Wv = X @ Wv + X @ Av @ Bv
233	
234	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
235	    See our blogpost for more details.
236	
237	    dC/dWq = X.T @ D(Wq)
238	    dC/dWk = X.T @ D(Wk)
239	    dC/dWv = X.T @ D(Wv)
240	    We then sum them all find dC/dX
241	
242	    ### Q projection LoRA weights
243	    dC/dAq =       X.T @ D(Wq) @ B.T
244	    dC/dBq = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wq)
245	
246	    ### K projection LoRA weights
247	    dC/dAk =       X.T @ D(Wk) @ B.T
248	    dC/dBk = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wk)
249	
250	    ### V projection LoRA weights
251	    dC/dAv =       X.T @ D(Wv) @ B.T
252	    dC/dBv = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wv)
253	    """
254	    @staticmethod
255	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
256	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
257	                QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS,
258	                KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS,
259	                VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS,
260	                inplace = True):
261	        dtype = X.dtype
262	
263	        Q = matmul_lora(X, QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS)
264	        K = matmul_lora(X, KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS)
265	        V = matmul_lora(X, VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS)
266	
267	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
268	            QW, QW_quant, QS,
269	            KW, KW_quant, KS,
270	            VW, VW_quant, VS,
271	        )
272	        ctx.save_for_backward(X, QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB,)
273	        ctx.inplace = inplace
274	        return Q, K, V
275	    pass
276	
277	    @staticmethod
278	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
279	    def backward(ctx, dQ, dK, dV):
280	        QW, QW_quant, QS, KW, KW_quant, KS, VW, VW_quant, VS = \
281	            ctx.custom_saved_tensors
282	        X, QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB, = ctx.saved_tensors
283	
284	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
285	        dQ = dQ.view(-1, dQ.shape[-1])
286	        dK = dK.reshape(-1, dK.shape[-1]) # view doesn't work on K.T
287	        dV = dV.view(-1, dV.shape[-1])
288	        X  = X .view(-1, X .shape[-1])
289	        dtype = X.dtype
290	
291	        QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB = \
292	            QA.to(dtype), QB.to(dtype), KA.to(dtype), KB.to(dtype), VA.to(dtype), VB.to(dtype)
293	
294	        QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB = \
295	            QA.t(), QB.t(), KA.t(), KB.t(), VA.t(), VB.t()
296	
297	        ### Weight projection LoRA weights
298	        # See our blogpost for more details.
299	        d_QA = torch.empty_like(QA)
300	        d_QB = torch.empty_like(QB)
301	        d_KA = torch.empty_like(KA)
302	        d_KB = torch.empty_like(KB)
303	        d_VA = torch.empty_like(VA)
304	        d_VB = torch.empty_like(VB)
305	
306	        # Q Projection
307	        # d_QA = X.t() @ (dQ @ QB.t())
308	        # d_QB = (QA.t() @ X.t()) @ dQ
309	        # d_QA *= QS
310	        # d_QB *= QS
311	        d_QA.addmm_(X.t(), dQ @ QB.t(), alpha = QS, beta = 0)
312	        d_QB.addmm_(QA.t() @ X.t(), dQ, alpha = QS, beta = 0)
313	
314	        # K Projection
315	        # d_KA = X.t() @ (dK @ KB.t())
316	        # d_KB = (KA.t() @ X.t()) @ dK
317	        # d_KA *= KS
318	        # d_KB *= KS
319	        d_KA.addmm_(X.t(), dK @ KB.t(), alpha = KS, beta = 0)
320	        d_KB.addmm_(KA.t() @ X.t(), dK, alpha = KS, beta = 0)
321	
322	        # V Projection
323	        # d_VA = X.t() @ (dV @ VB.t())
324	        # d_VB = (VA.t() @ X.t()) @ dV
325	        # d_VA *= VS
326	        # d_VB *= VS
327	        d_VA.addmm_(X.t(), dV @ VB.t(), alpha = VS, beta = 0)
328	        d_VB.addmm_(VA.t() @ X.t(), dV, alpha = VS, beta = 0)
329	
330	        # Combine derivatives to find dX
331	        # dQ
332	        QW = fast_dequantize(QW.t(), QW_quant)
333	        dX = torch.matmul(dQ, QW.t(), out = X if ctx.inplace else None)
334	        del QW
335	        # dX += (dQ @ QB.to(dtype).t() @ (QS * QA.to(dtype).t()))
336	        dX.addmm_(dQ @ QB.t(), QA.t(), alpha = QS)
337	
338	        # dK
339	        KW = fast_dequantize(KW.t(), KW_quant)
340	        # dX += dK @ KW.t()
341	        dX.addmm_(dK, KW.t())
342	        del KW
343	        # dX += dK @ KB.to(dtype).t() @ (KS * KA.to(dtype).t())
344	        dX.addmm_(dK @ KB.t(), KA.t(), alpha = KS)
345	
346	        # dV
347	        VW = fast_dequantize(VW.t(), VW_quant)
348	        # dX += dV @ VW.t()
349	        dX.addmm_(dV, VW.t())
350	        del VW
351	        # dX += dV @ VB.to(dtype).t() @ (VS * VA.to(dtype).t())
352	        dX.addmm_(dV @ VB.t(), VA.t(), alpha = VS)
353	
354	        # QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS,
355	        # KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS,
356	        # VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS,
357	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
358	            None, None, d_QA.t(), d_QB.t(), None, \
359	            None, None, d_KA.t(), d_KB.t(), None, \
360	            None, None, d_VA.t(), d_VB.t(), None, \
361	            None,
362	    pass
363	pass
364

369	    KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS = get_lora_parameters(self.k_proj)
370	    VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS = get_lora_parameters(self.v_proj)
371	    Q, K, V = LoRA_QKV.apply(X,
372	        QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS,

377	    return Q, K, V

381	class LoRA_W(torch.autograd.Function):
382	    """
383	    ### LoRA weights
384	    Wq = Wq + Aq @ Bq
385	    Wk = Wk + Ak @ Bk
386	    Wv = Wv + Av @ Bv
387	    Q = X @ Wq = X @ Wq + X @ Aq @ Bq
388	    K = X @ Wk = X @ Wk + X @ Ak @ Bk
389	    V = X @ Wv = X @ Wv + X @ Av @ Bv
390	
391	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
392	    dC/dWq = X.T @ D(Wq)
393	    dC/dWk = X.T @ D(Wk)
394	    dC/dWv = X.T @ D(Wv)
395	
396	    ### Q projection LoRA weights
397	    dC/dAq =       X.T @ D(Wq) @ B.T
398	    dC/dBq = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wq)
399	
400	    ### K projection LoRA weights
401	    dC/dAk =       X.T @ D(Wk) @ B.T
402	    dC/dBk = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wk)
403	
404	    ### V projection LoRA weights
405	    dC/dAv =       X.T @ D(Wv) @ B.T
406	    dC/dBv = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wv)
407	    """
408	    @staticmethod
409	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
410	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
411	                W, W_quant, A, B, S):
412	        dtype = X.dtype
413	        XW = matmul_lora(X, W, W_quant, A, B, S)
414	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (W, W_quant, S,)
415	        ctx.save_for_backward(A, B, X)
416	        return XW
417	    pass
418	
419	    @staticmethod
420	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
421	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
422	        W, W_quant, S = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
423	        A, B, X = ctx.saved_tensors
424	
425	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
426	        dY = dY.reshape(-1, dY.shape[-1]) # Must be reshape
427	        X  = X .reshape(-1, X .shape[-1]) # Must be reshape
428	        dtype = X.dtype
429	
430	        A, B = A.to(dtype), B.to(dtype)
431	
432	        A, B = A.t(), B.t()
433	
434	        d_A = torch.empty_like(A)
435	        d_B = torch.empty_like(B)
436	
437	        ### Weight projection LoRA weights
438	        # Weight projection
439	        # d_A = X.t() @ (dY @ B.t())
440	        # d_B = (A.t() @ X.t()) @ dY
441	        # d_A *= S
442	        # d_B *= S
443	        d_A.addmm_(X.t(), dY @ B.t(), alpha = S, beta = 0)
444	        d_B.addmm_(A.t() @ X.t(), dY, alpha = S, beta = 0)
445	
446	        # Get derivative for dX
447	        W = fast_dequantize(W.t(), W_quant)
448	        dX = dY @ W.t()
449	        del W
450	        # dX += dY @ B.to(dtype).t() @ (S * A.to(dtype).t())
451	        dX.addmm_(dY @ B.t(), A.t(), alpha = S)
452	
453	        # W, W_quant, A, B, S
454	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
455	            None, None, d_A.t(), d_B.t(), None
456	    pass
457	pass
458

462	    OW, OW_quant, OA, OB, OS = get_lora_parameters(self.o_proj)
463	    O = LoRA_W.apply(X, OW, OW_quant, OA, OB, OS)
464	    return O
465	pass

468	IDENTITY_DROPOUT = torch.nn.Identity
469	@torch._disable_dynamo
470	def fast_lora_forward(self, x: torch.Tensor, *args, **kwargs) -> torch.Tensor:
471	    raise NotImplementedError(
472	        "Unsloth: Currently not supported yet - reshaping done incorrectly"
473	    )
474	    self._check_forward_args(x, *args, **kwargs)
475	    adapter_names = kwargs.pop("adapter_names", None)
476	
477	    if self.disable_adapters:
478	        if self.merged:
479	            self.unmerge()
480	        result = self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
481	    elif adapter_names is not None:
482	        result = self._mixed_batch_forward(x, *args, adapter_names=adapter_names, **kwargs)
483	    elif self.merged:
484	        result = self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
485	    else:
486	        # Fastpath
487	        if len(self.active_adapters) == 1:
488	            active_adapter = self.active_adapters[0]
489	            if active_adapter not in self.lora_A.keys(): return self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
490	
491	            dropout = self.lora_dropout[active_adapter]
492	            if isinstance(dropout, IDENTITY_DROPOUT) and not self.use_dora[active_adapter]:
493	                lora_A = self.lora_A[active_adapter].weight
494	                lora_B = self.lora_B[active_adapter].weight
495	                scaling = self.scaling[active_adapter]
496	                W = self.base_layer.weight
497	                return LoRA_W.apply(x, W, QUANT_STATE(W), lora_A, lora_B, scaling)
498	            pass
499	        pass
500	
501	        result = self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
502	        # As per Tim Dettmers, for 4bit, we need to defensively clone here.
503	        # The reason is that in some cases, an error can occur that backprop
504	        # does not work on a manipulated view. This issue may be solved with
505	        # newer PyTorch versions but this would need extensive testing to be
506	        # sure.
507	        result = result.clone()
508	
509	        for active_adapter in self.active_adapters:
510	            if active_adapter not in self.lora_A.keys():
511	                continue
512	            lora_A = self.lora_A[active_adapter]
513	            lora_B = self.lora_B[active_adapter]
514	            dropout = self.lora_dropout[active_adapter]
515	            scaling = self.scaling[active_adapter]
516	
517	            requires_conversion = not torch.is_autocast_enabled()
518	            if requires_conversion:
519	                expected_dtype = result.dtype
520	                x = x.to(lora_A.weight.dtype)
521	
522	            if not self.use_dora[active_adapter]:
523	                result = result + lora_B(lora_A(dropout(x))) * scaling
524	            else:
525	                if isinstance(dropout, torch.nn.Identity) or not self.training:
526	                    base_result = result
527	                else:
528	                    x = dropout(x)
529	                    base_result = None
530	
531	                result = result + self.lora_magnitude_vector[active_adapter](
532	                    x,
533	                    lora_A=lora_A,
534	                    lora_B=lora_B,
535	                    scaling=scaling,
536	                    base_layer=self.get_base_layer(),
537	                    base_result=base_result,
538	                )
539	            if requires_conversion:
540	                result = result.to(expected_dtype)
541	
542	    return result
543	pass
544

1951	def unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf(
1952	    self,
1953	    repo_id              : str,
1954	    tokenizer            = None,
1955	    quantization_method  : str = "fast_quantized",
1956	    first_conversion     : str = None,
1957	    use_temp_dir         : Optional[bool] = None,
1958	    commit_message       : Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
1959	    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
1960	    token                : Union[bool, str, None] = None,
1961	    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str, None] = "5GB",
1962	    create_pr            : bool = False,
1963	    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
1964	    revision             : str = None,
1965	    commit_description   : str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
1966	    tags                 : Optional[List[str]] = None,
1967	    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
1968	    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.85,
1969	):
1970	    """
1971	        Same as .push_to_hub(...) except 4bit weights are auto
1972	        converted to float16 then converted to GGUF / llama.cpp format.
1973	
1974	        Choose for `quantization_method` to be:
1975	        "not_quantized"  : "Recommended. Fast conversion. Slow inference, big files.",
1976	        "fast_quantized" : "Recommended. Fast conversion. OK inference, OK file size.",
1977	        "quantized"      : "Recommended. Slow conversion. Fast inference, small files.",
1978	        "f32"     : "Not recommended. Retains 100% accuracy, but super slow and memory hungry.",
1979	        "f16"     : "Fastest conversion + retains 100% accuracy. Slow and memory hungry.",
1980	        "q8_0"    : "Fast conversion. High resource use, but generally acceptable.",
1981	        "q4_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q4_K",

2276	def save_to_gguf_generic(
2277	    model,
2278	    save_directory,
2279	    tokenizer,
2280	    quantization_method = None,
2281	    quantization_type = "Q8_0",
2282	    repo_id = None,
2283	    token = None,
2284	):
2285	    if token is None and repo_id is not None: token = get_token()
2286	    if repo_id is not None and token is None:
2287	        raise RuntimeError("Unsloth: Please specify a token for uploading!")
2288	
2289	    if not os.path.exists(os.path.join("llama.cpp", "unsloth_convert_hf_to_gguf.py")):
2290	        install_llama_cpp(just_clone_repo = True)
2291	    pass
2292	
2293	    # Use old style quantization_method
2294	    new_quantization_methods = []
2295	    if quantization_method is not None:
2296	        # Convert quantization_method to list
2297	        if   isinstance(quantization_method, list):  pass
2298	        elif isinstance(quantization_method, str):   quantization_method = [ quantization_method, ]
2299	        elif isinstance(quantization_method, tuple): quantization_method = list(quantization_method)
2300	        else:
2301	            raise TypeError("Unsloth: quantization_method can only be a string or a list of strings")
2302	        pass
2303	        for i, quant_method in enumerate(quantization_method):
2304	            quant_method = quant_method.lower()
2305	            if   quant_method == "not_quantized":  quant_method = "f16"
2306	            elif quant_method == "fast_quantized": quant_method = "q8_0"
2307	            elif quant_method == "quantized":      quant_method = "q4_k_m"
2308	            elif quant_method is None:             quant_method = "q8_0"
2309	            new_quantization_methods.append(quant_method.lower())
2310	        pass
2311	    else:
2312	        new_quantization_methods.append(quantization_type.lower())
2313	    # Check if wrong method
2314	    for quant_method in new_quantization_methods:
2315	        if quant_method not in ALLOWED_QUANTS.keys():
2316	            error = f"Unsloth: Quant method = [{quant_method}] not supported. Choose from below:\n"
2317	            for key, value in ALLOWED_QUANTS.items():
2318	                error += f"[{key}] => {value}\n"
2319	            raise RuntimeError(error)
2320	        pass
2321	    pass
2322	
2323	    # Go through all types and save individually - somewhat inefficient
2324	    # since we save F16 / BF16 multiple times
2325	    for quantization_type in new_quantization_methods:
2326	        metadata = _convert_to_gguf(
2327	            save_directory,
2328	            print_output = True,
2329	            quantization_type = quantization_type,
2330	        )

2416	    tokenizer            = None,

2419	    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
2420	    is_main_process      : bool = True,

2434

2444	        )
2445	    pass
2446	
2447	    arguments = dict(locals())
2448	    arguments["model"] = self
2449	    del arguments["self"]
2450	    unsloth_generic_save(**arguments)
2451	    for _ in range(3):
2452	        gc.collect()
2453	pass

159	def _merge_lora(layer, name):
160	
161	    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
162	    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
163	        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
164	        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
165	        if quant_state is not None:
166	            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
167	            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
168	        else:
169	            dtype = W.dtype
170	        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
171	        # W = W.t()
172	
173	        if A is not None:
174	            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
175	            # W += sAB
176	            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
177	            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
178	            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
179	            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
180	            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
181	                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
182	        pass
183	        W = W.t().to(dtype)
184	    else:
185	        W = layer.weight
186	    return W, bias

276	    @torch.inference_mode
277	    def fast_dequantize(W, quant_state = None, out = None, use_global_buffer = False):
278	        # TODO: After adding XPU BNB support, check this function
279	        if quant_state is None: return W
280	        if type(quant_state) is not list:
281	            # New quant_state as a class
282	            # https://github.com/TimDettmers/bitsandbytes/pull/763/files
283	            absmax     = quant_state.absmax
284	            shape      = quant_state.shape
285	            dtype      = quant_state.dtype
286	            blocksize  = quant_state.blocksize
287	            offset     = quant_state.offset
288	            state2     = quant_state.state2
289	            absmax2    = state2.absmax
290	            code2      = state2.code
291	            blocksize2 = state2.blocksize
292	        else:
293	            # Old quant_state as a list of lists
294	            absmax, shape, dtype, blocksize, compressed_stats, _, _ = quant_state
295	            offset, state2 = compressed_stats
296	            absmax2, code2, blocksize2, _, _, _, _ = state2
297	        pass
298	        global XPU_STREAMS
299	        device = W.device
300	        device_index = device.index
301	        XPU_STREAM = XPU_STREAMS[device_index]
302	
303	        n_elements_absmax = absmax.numel()
304	        # Create weight matrix
305	        if use_global_buffer:
306	
307	            # Use same buffers for faster inference
308	            size = shape[0]*shape[1]
309	            global WEIGHT_BUFFERS
310	            global ABSMAX_BUFFERS
311	            WEIGHT_BUFFER = WEIGHT_BUFFERS[device_index]
312	            ABSMAX_BUFFER = ABSMAX_BUFFERS[device_index]
313	            if WEIGHT_BUFFER is None:
314	                WEIGHT_BUFFERS[device_index] = WEIGHT_BUFFER = torch_empty(size, dtype = dtype, device = device, requires_grad = False)
315	                ABSMAX_BUFFERS[device_index] = ABSMAX_BUFFER = torch_empty(n_elements_absmax, dtype = torch.float32, device = device, requires_grad = False)
316	
317	            if size > WEIGHT_BUFFER.numel(): WEIGHT_BUFFER.resize_(size)
318	            if n_elements_absmax > ABSMAX_BUFFER.numel(): ABSMAX_BUFFER.resize_(n_elements_absmax)
319	
320	            out = WEIGHT_BUFFER[:size].view(shape)
321	            out_absmax = ABSMAX_BUFFER[:n_elements_absmax]
322	        else:
323	            if out is None:
324	                out = torch_empty(shape, dtype = dtype, device = device, requires_grad = False)
325	            else:
326	                assert(out.shape == shape)
327	                assert(out.dtype == dtype)
328	            out_absmax = torch_empty(n_elements_absmax, dtype = torch_float32, device = device, requires_grad = False)
329	        pass
330	
331	        # NF4 dequantization of statistics
332	        ptr_out_absmax = get_ptr(out_absmax)
333	        with torch_gpu_device(device):
334	            cdequantize_blockwise_fp32(
335	                get_ptr(code2), get_ptr(absmax), get_ptr(absmax2), ptr_out_absmax,
336	                ctypes_c_int(blocksize2), ctypes_c_int(n_elements_absmax), XPU_STREAM
337	            )
338	            out_absmax += offset
339	
340	            # Dequantize W
341	            fx = cdequantize_blockwise_fp16_nf4 if dtype == torch_float16 else \
342	                 cdequantize_blockwise_bf16_nf4
343	            fx(get_ptr(None), get_ptr(W), ptr_out_absmax, get_ptr(out),
344	               ctypes_c_int(blocksize), ctypes_c_int(out.numel()), XPU_STREAM,)
345	        pass
346	        # Careful returning transposed data
347	        is_transposed = (True if W.shape[0] == 1 else False)
348	        return out.t() if is_transposed else out
349	    pass

77	def _create_unsloth_optimizer(
78	    model,
79	    optimizer_cls,
80	    optimizer_kwargs,
81	    embedding_lr = 5e-5,
82	):
83	    lr = optimizer_kwargs["lr"]
84	    weight_decay = optimizer_kwargs.get("weight_decay", 0.0)
85	
86	    param_groups = \
87	    {
88	        "non_embeddings" : {},
89	        "embeddings"     : {},
90	    }
91	
92	    for name, param in model.named_parameters():
93	        if not param.requires_grad: continue
94	        if name.endswith("modules_to_save.default.weight"):
95	            partial_name = name[:-len(".modules_to_save.default.weight")]
96	            partial_name = partial_name[partial_name.rfind(".")+1:]
97	            print(f"Unsloth: Setting lr = {embedding_lr:.2e} instead of {lr:.2e} for {partial_name}.")
98	            param_groups["embeddings"]    [name] = param
99	        else:
100	            param_groups["non_embeddings"][name] = param
101	        pass
102	    pass
103	
104	    optimizer_grouped_parameters = [
105	        {
106	            "params"       : list(param_groups["non_embeddings"].values()),
107	            "weight_decay" : weight_decay,
108	            "lr"           : lr,
109	        },
110	        {
111	            "params"       : list(param_groups["embeddings"].values()),
112	            "weight_decay" : weight_decay,
113	            "lr"           : embedding_lr,
114	        },
115	    ]
116	    optimizer = optimizer_cls(optimizer_grouped_parameters, **optimizer_kwargs)
117	    return optimizer
118	pass

121	class UnslothTrainer(SFTTrainer):
122	    def create_optimizer(self):
123	        embedding_learning_rate = getattr(self.args, "embedding_learning_rate", None)
124	        if embedding_learning_rate is None: return super().create_optimizer()
125	
126	        if self.optimizer is None:

139	# From `trl>=0.13.0`, they changed how to pass several params to the trainer
140	# We need to patch to make the transition smooth
141	def _backwards_compatible_trainer(trainer_class, config_class):
142	    original_init = trainer_class.__init__
143	    
144	    @wraps(original_init)
145	    def new_init(self, *args, **kwargs):
146	        # All Trainer tokenizer are now called processing_class
147	        trainer_params = set(inspect.signature(original_init).parameters.keys())
148	
149	        if "processing_class" in trainer_params and "tokenizer" in kwargs:
150	            kwargs["processing_class"] = kwargs.pop("tokenizer")
151	        pass
152	
153	        if ("args" in kwargs) and (Version(trl.__version__) >= Version("0.13.0.dev0")):
154	            training_args = kwargs.pop("args", None)
155	
156	            # Get parameters that Trainer.__init__ actually expects
157	            trainer_params.remove('self')
158	            trainer_params.remove('args')
159	
160	            # Get fields that should be passed to Config init
161	            config_fields = {
162	                field.name: field for field in dataclasses.fields(config_class) 
163	                if field.init
164	            }
165	            
166	            # Create config dict with valid fields from training_args
167	            config_dict = {
168	                name: getattr(training_args, name)
169	                for name in config_fields
170	                if hasattr(training_args, name)
171	            }
172	
173	            # Get parameters that exist in Config but not in TrainingArguments
174	            from transformers import TrainingArguments
175	            moved_params = \
176	                set(inspect.signature(config_class)     .parameters.keys()) - \
177	                set(inspect.signature(TrainingArguments).parameters.keys())
178	            
179	            # Separate kwargs into trainer kwargs and config kwargs
180	            trainer_kwargs = {}
181	            additional_config_kwargs = {}
182	
183	            for key, value in kwargs.items():
184	                if key in trainer_params: trainer_kwargs[key] = value
185	                elif key in moved_params or key in config_fields:
186	                    additional_config_kwargs[key] = value
187	                else:
188	                    additional_config_kwargs[key] = value
189	                pass
190	            pass
191	
192	            # Update config_dict with additional kwargs
193	            config_dict.update(additional_config_kwargs)
194	
195	            # Create Config with all the collected parameters
196	            # Reinitialising config class with parameters (that were none initially but populated on first init)
197	            # causes the 2nd init to fail as there are mutual exclusive checks on pairs of parameters.
198	            # Refer: https://github.com/huggingface/trl/blob/main/trl/trainer/grpo_config.py#L499-L502 for example
199	            # So we only create config class if the previous init was not TrainingArguments
200	            if not isinstance(training_args, TrainingArguments):
201	                config = config_class(**config_dict)
202	            else:
203	                config = training_args
204	
205	            # Reconstruct kwargs for Trainer
206	            kwargs = trainer_kwargs
207	            kwargs["args"] = config
208	        pass
209	        original_init(self, *args, **kwargs)
210	    pass
211	    return new_init
212	pass
213	
214	
215	def _patch_trl_trainer():
216	    import trl
217	    if hasattr(trl, "__UNSLOTH_BACKWARDS_COMPATIBLE__"): return
218	    if Version(trl.__version__) <= Version("0.11.0"): return
219	
220	    import trl.trainer
221	    trl_classes = dir(trl.trainer)
222	    trl_trainers = set(x[:-len("Trainer")] for x in trl_classes if x.endswith("Trainer"))
223	    trl_configs  = set(x[:-len("Config")]  for x in trl_classes if x.endswith("Config"))
224	    trl_classes = list(trl_trainers & trl_configs)
225	
226	    for x in trl_classes:
227	        try:    exec(f"trl.{x}Trainer.__init__ = _backwards_compatible_trainer(trl.{x}Trainer, trl.{x}Config)", globals())
228	        except: continue
229	    pass
230	
231	    trl.__UNSLOTH_BACKWARDS_COMPATIBLE__ = True
232	pass

25	def get_model_info(
26	    model_id: str, properties: list[str] = ["safetensors", "lastModified"]
27	) -> ModelInfo:
28	    """
29	    Get the model info for a specific model.
30	
31	    properties: list[str] = See https://huggingface.co/docs/huggingface_hub/api-ref/hf_hub/hf_api/model_info
32	    Default properties: ["safetensors", "lastModified"], only retrieves minimal information.
33	    Set to None to retrieve the full model information.
34	    """
35	    global _HFAPI
36	    if _HFAPI is None:
37	        _HFAPI = HfApi()
38	    try:
39	        model_info: ModelInfo = _HFAPI.model_info(model_id, expand=properties)
40	    except Exception as e:
41	        print(f"Error getting model info for {model_id}: {e}")
42	        model_info = None
43	    return model_info

46	def list_models(
47	    properties: list[str] = None,
48	    full: bool = False,
49	    sort: str = "downloads",
50	    author: str = "unsloth",
51	    search: str = None,
52	    limit: int = 10,
53	) -> list[ModelInfo]:
54	    """
55	    Retrieve model information from the Hugging Face Hub.
56	
57	    properties: list[str] = See https://huggingface.co/docs/huggingface_hub/api-ref/hf_hub/hf_api/list_models
58	    full: bool = Whether to retrieve the full model information, if True properties will be ignored.
59	    sort: str = The sort order.
60	    author: str = The author of the model.
61	    search: str = The search query for filtering models.
62	
63	    """
64	    global _HFAPI
65	    if _HFAPI is None:
66	        _HFAPI = HfApi()
67	    if full:
68	        properties = None
69	
70	    models: list[ModelInfo] = _HFAPI.list_models(
71	        author=author,
72	        search=search,
73	        sort=sort,
74	        limit=limit,
75	        expand=properties,
76	        full=full,
77	    )
78	    return models

6	class QuantType(Enum):
7	    BNB = "bnb"
8	    UNSLOTH = "unsloth" # dynamic 4-bit quantization
9	    GGUF = "GGUF"
10	    NONE = "none"
11	    BF16 = "bf16" # only for Deepseek V3
12	
13	# Tags for Hugging Face model paths
14	BNB_QUANTIZED_TAG = "bnb-4bit"
15	UNSLOTH_DYNAMIC_QUANT_TAG = "unsloth" + "-" + BNB_QUANTIZED_TAG
16	GGUF_TAG = "GGUF"
17	BF16_TAG = "bf16"
18	
19	QUANT_TAG_MAP = {
20	    QuantType.BNB: BNB_QUANTIZED_TAG,
21	    QuantType.UNSLOTH: UNSLOTH_DYNAMIC_QUANT_TAG,
22	    QuantType.GGUF: GGUF_TAG,
23	    QuantType.NONE: None,
24	    QuantType.BF16: BF16_TAG,
25	}

27	# NOTE: models registered with org="unsloth" and QUANT_TYPE.NONE are aliases of QUANT_TYPE.UNSLOTH
28	@dataclass
29	class ModelInfo:

44	            self.size,
45	            self.quant_type,

49	    @staticmethod

51	        if instruct_tag:
52	            key = "-".join([key, instruct_tag])
53	        return key
54	
55	    @staticmethod
56	    def append_quant_type(
57	        key: str, quant_type: QuantType = None
58	    ):
59	        if quant_type != QuantType.NONE:
60	            key = "-".join([key, QUANT_TAG_MAP[quant_type]])
61	        return key
62	
63	    @classmethod
64	    def construct_model_name(cls, base_name, version, size, quant_type, instruct_tag, key=""):
65	        key = cls.append_instruct_tag(key, instruct_tag)
66	        key = cls.append_quant_type(key, quant_type)
67	        return key
68	
69	    @property
70	    def model_path(
71	        self,
72	    ) -> str:
73	        return f"{self.org}/{self.name}"
74

76	@dataclass
77	class ModelMeta:
78	    org: str
79	    base_name: str
80	    model_version: str
81	    model_info_cls: type[ModelInfo]
82	    model_sizes: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
83	    instruct_tags: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
84	    quant_types: list[QuantType] | dict[str, list[QuantType]] = field(default_factory=list)
85	    is_multimodal: bool = False
86	
87	
88	MODEL_REGISTRY: dict[str, ModelInfo] = {}
89	
90	
91	def register_model(
92	    model_info_cls: ModelInfo,
93	    org: str,
94	    base_name: str,
95	    version: str,
96	    size: int,
97	    instruct_tag: str = None,
98	    quant_type: QuantType = None,
99	    is_multimodal: bool = False,
100	    name: str = None,
101	):
102	    name = name or model_info_cls.construct_model_name(
103	        base_name=base_name,
104	        version=version,
105	        size=size,
106	        quant_type=quant_type,
107	        instruct_tag=instruct_tag,
108	    )
109	    key = f"{org}/{name}"
110	
111	    if key in MODEL_REGISTRY:
112	        raise ValueError(f"Model {key} already registered, current keys: {MODEL_REGISTRY.keys()}")
113	
114	    MODEL_REGISTRY[key] = model_info_cls(
115	        org=org,
116	        base_name=base_name,
117	        version=version,
118	        size=size,
119	        is_multimodal=is_multimodal,
120	        instruct_tag=instruct_tag,
121	        quant_type=quant_type,
122	        name=name,
123	    )
124

126	def _check_model_info(model_id: str, properties: list[str] = ["lastModified"]):
127	    from huggingface_hub import HfApi
128	    from huggingface_hub import ModelInfo as HfModelInfo
129	    from huggingface_hub.utils import RepositoryNotFoundError
130	
131	    api = HfApi()
132	
133	    try:
134	        model_info: HfModelInfo = api.model_info(model_id, expand=properties)
135	    except Exception as e:
136	        if isinstance(e, RepositoryNotFoundError):
137	            warnings.warn(f"{model_id} not found on Hugging Face")
138	            model_info = None
139	        else:
140	            raise e
141	    return model_info
142	
143	
144	def _register_models(model_meta: ModelMeta, include_original_model: bool = False):
145	    org = model_meta.org
146	    base_name = model_meta.base_name
147	    instruct_tags = model_meta.instruct_tags
148	    model_version = model_meta.model_version
149	    model_sizes = model_meta.model_sizes
150	    is_multimodal = model_meta.is_multimodal
151	    quant_types = model_meta.quant_types
152	    model_info_cls = model_meta.model_info_cls
153	
154	    for size in model_sizes:
155	        for instruct_tag in instruct_tags:
156	            # Handle quant types per model size
157	            if isinstance(quant_types, dict):
158	                _quant_types = quant_types[size]
159	            else:
160	                _quant_types = quant_types
161	            for quant_type in _quant_types:
162	                # NOTE: models registered with org="unsloth" and QUANT_TYPE.NONE are aliases of QUANT_TYPE.UNSLOTH
163	                _org = "unsloth" # unsloth models -- these are all quantized versions of the original model
164	                register_model(
165	                    model_info_cls=model_info_cls,
166	                    org=_org,
167	                    base_name=base_name,
168	                    version=model_version,
169	                    size=size,
170	                    instruct_tag=instruct_tag,
171	                    quant_type=quant_type,
172	                    is_multimodal=is_multimodal,
173	                )
174	            # include original model from releasing organization
175	            if include_original_model:
176	                register_model(
177	                    model_info_cls=model_info_cls,
178	                    org=org,
179	                    base_name=base_name,
180	                    version=model_version,
181	                    size=size,
182	                    instruct_tag=instruct_tag,
183	                    quant_type=QuantType.NONE,
184	                    is_multimodal=is_multimodal,
185	                )

25	def search_models(org: str = None, base_name: str = None, version: str = None, size: str = None, quant_types: list[QuantType] = None, search_pattern: str = None) -> list[ModelInfo]:
26	    """
27	    Get model info from the registry.
28	
29	    See registry.ModelInfo for more fields.
30	
31	    If search_pattern is provided, the full model path will be matched against the pattern, where the model path is the model_id on huggingface hub.
32	
33	    """
34	    if not _ARE_MODELS_REGISTERED:
35	        register_models()
36	    
37	    model_infos = MODEL_REGISTRY.values()
38	    if org:
39	        model_infos = [model_info for model_info in model_infos if model_info.org == org]
40	    if base_name:
41	        model_infos = [model_info for model_info in model_infos if model_info.base_name == base_name]
42	    if version:
43	        model_infos = [model_info for model_info in model_infos if model_info.version == version]
44	    if size:
45	        model_infos = [model_info for model_info in model_infos if model_info.size == size]
46	    if quant_types:
47	        model_infos = [model_info for model_info in model_infos if any(model_info.quant_type == quant_type for quant_type in quant_types)]
48	    if search_pattern:
49	        model_infos = [model_info for model_info in model_infos if search_pattern in model_info.model_path]
50	    
51	    return model_infos

137	def _free_cached_model(model):
138	    from huggingface_hub import scan_cache_dir
139	    cached_repos = list(scan_cache_dir().repos)
140	
141	    # Go through every cached repo, and delete the one that matches the model we want to save.
142	    # Can save 4GB of disk space - useful for Kaggle systems.
143	    for cached_repo in cached_repos:
144	        if cached_repo.repo_id == model.config._name_or_path:
145	            remove_cache_commit = list(cached_repo.revisions)[0].commit_hash
146	            delete_strategy = scan_cache_dir().delete_revisions(remove_cache_commit,)
147	
148	            logger.warning_once(
149	                "Unsloth: Will remove a cached repo with size " + \
150	                delete_strategy.expected_freed_size_str,
151	            )
152	
153	            delete_strategy.execute()
154	        pass
155	    pass

159	def _merge_lora(layer, name):
160	
161	    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
162	    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
163	        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
164	        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
165	        if quant_state is not None:
166	            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
167	            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
168	        else:
169	            dtype = W.dtype
170	        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
171	        # W = W.t()
172	
173	        if A is not None:
174	            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
175	            # W += sAB
176	            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
177	            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
178	            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
179	            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
180	            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
181	                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
182	        pass
183	        W = W.t().to(dtype)
184	    else:
185	        W = layer.weight
186	    return W, bias
187	pass

## Comprehensive Report: Unsloth Save and Export Methods

I've completed a thorough search of the unsloth codebase for all save and export implementations. Here's the detailed findings:

### 1. Core Save/Export Methods

**File: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395345315-bq9aoq/unsloth/save.py`**

#### A. Main Save Function (Foundation for all save operations)

**Function: `unsloth_save_model`** (Line 205)
- **Signature:**
```python
def unsloth_save_model(
    model, tokenizer,
    save_directory: Union[str, os.PathLike],
    save_method: str = "lora",  # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    push_to_hub: bool = False,
    token: Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    is_main_process: bool = True,
    state_dict: Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function: Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size: Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization: bool = True,
    variant: Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format: bool = True,
    use_temp_dir: Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message: Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
    private: Optional[bool] = None,
    create_pr: bool = False,
    revision: str = None,
    commit_description: str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
    tags: List[str] = None,
    temporary_location: str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage: float = 0.9,
)
```

- **Purpose:** Core function handling all save operations with three save methods:
  - `"lora"`: Saves LoRA adapters only (no merging)
  - `"merged_16bit"`: Merges LoRA into float16 weights (ideal for GGUF/llama.cpp)
  - `"merged_4bit"`: Merges LoRA into int4 weights (for DPO/HF inference)

- **Key Operations:**
  - Validates save_method (lines 283-291)
  - Merges 4bit weights with LoRA if `save_method == "merged_4bit"` (lines 293-302) using `model.merge_and_unload()`
  - Calls internal `_merge_lora()` function (line 159) to merge LoRA weights layer-by-layer
  - Memory management: Uses GPU/RAM/disk intelligently based on availability (lines 562-603)
  - Iterates through model layers (line 567-602) and merges each weight with `_merge_lora()`
  - Handles HuggingFace Hub uploads if `push_to_hub=True`

#### B. LoRA Merge Helper Function

**Function: `_merge_lora`** (Line 159)
- **Signature:**
```python
def _merge_lora(layer, name)
```
- **Purpose:** Merges LoRA weights into base weights for individual layers
- **Operations:**
  - Extracts LoRA parameters using `get_lora_parameters_bias()`
  - If quantized (4bit): dequantizes using `fast_dequantize()`
  - Performs merge using matrix multiplication: `W.addmm_(A.t(), B.t(), alpha=s)` (line 176)
  - Returns merged weights in original dtype

---

### 2. Model-Level Save Methods (Attached via Patching)

**Function: `unsloth_save_pretrained_merged`** (Line 1321)
- **Signature:**
```python
def unsloth_save_pretrained_merged(
    self,

    tokenizer=None,
    save_method: str = "merged_16bit",  # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]

    ... (standard save parameters)
)
```
- **Purpose:** Model method wrapper for merged saving
- **Implementation:** Delegates to `unsloth_save_model()` (line 1358)

**Function: `unsloth_push_to_hub_merged`** (Line 1364)
- **Signature:**
```python

    self,
    repo_id: str,
    tokenizer=None,
    save_method: str = "merged_16bit",
    ... (standard hub parameters)
)
```
- **Purpose:** Pushes merged model to HuggingFace Hub
- **Implementation:** Calls `unsloth_save_model()` with `push_to_hub=True` (line 1404)

---

### 3. GGUF Export Methods

**Function: `unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf`** (Line 1768)
- **Signature:**
```python
def unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf(
    self,
    save_directory: Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer=None,
    quantization_method: str = "fast_quantized",

)
```
- **Purpose:** Saves model in GGUF format (llama.cpp compatible)
- **Quantization Options:** "not_quantized", "fast_quantized", "quantized", "f32", "f16", "q8_0", "q4_k_m", "q5_k_m", "q2_k", "q3_k_*", "q4_*", "q5_*", "q6_k"
- **Key Operations (lines 1819-1902):**
  - Merges model to float16 first (sets `save_method = "merged_16bit"`)
  - Non-blocking install of llama.cpp if missing
  - Calls `save_to_gguf()` (line 1899) for conversion
  - Creates Ollama modelfile if applicable
  - Fixes tokenizer BOS token issues

**Function: `unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf`** (Line 1951)
- **Signature:** Similar to `unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf` but for pushing to Hub
- **Key Operations:** Same as above but pushes GGUF files to HuggingFace Hub (line 2098-2099)

**Function: `save_to_gguf`** (Line 952)
- **Signature:**
```python

    model_type: str,

    quantization_method = "fast_quantized",  # Can be list
    first_conversion: str = None,
    _run_installer = None,
)
```
- **Purpose:** Core GGUF conversion using llama.cpp
- **Operations:**
  - Validates quantization methods against ALLOWED_QUANTS dictionary
  - Installs llama.cpp if needed (uses non-blocking subprocess)
  - Executes llama.cpp conversion script
  - Creates multiple quantized versions in parallel

---

### 4. GGML Export Methods (LoRA-specific)

**Function: `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub`** (Line 2143)
- **Signature:**

```python
    self,

    ... (hub parameters)
)
```
- **Purpose:** Converts LoRA adapters to GGML format and pushes to Hub
- **Operations (lines 2175-2209):**
  - Saves LoRA to temporary directory via `save_lora_to_custom_dir()`
  - Executes `llama.cpp/convert-lora-to-ggml.py`
  - Uploads resulting `ggml-adapter-model.bin` to HuggingFace Hub

**Function: `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally`** (Line 2211)
- **Signature:**
```python
def unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally(
    self,
    save_directory: str,
    tokenizer,
    temporary_location: str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage: float = 0.85,
)
```
- **Purpose:** Converts LoRA adapters to GGML format and saves locally
- **Operations:** Same as above but saves to provided directory instead of Hub

**Function: `save_lora_to_custom_dir`** (Line 2129)
- **Purpose:** Helper to save LoRA adapters to custom directory
- **Implementation:** Calls `unsloth_save_model()` with `save_method="lora"`

---

### 5. Generic/Vision Model Save Methods

**Function: `unsloth_generic_save`** (Line 2356)
- **Purpose:** Generic save for non-Llama/Mistral architectures
- **Implementation:** Delegates to `merge_and_overwrite_lora()` from unsloth_zoo

**Function: `unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged`** (Line 2413)
- **Purpose:** Generic model method for merged saves
- **Wrapper:** Calls `unsloth_generic_save()`

**Function: `unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged`** (Line 2456)
- **Purpose:** Generic Hub push for merged models
- **Wrapper:** Calls `unsloth_generic_save()` with `push_to_hub=True`

---

### 6. TorchAO Quantization Save

**Function: `unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao`** (Line 2502)
- **Signature:**
```python
def unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao(
    self,
    save_directory: Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer=None,
    torchao_config=None,
    push_to_hub: bool = False,
    token: Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
)
```
- **Purpose:** Quantizes model with torchao and saves checkpoint
- **Operations (lines 2517-2558):**
  - First merges LoRA to merged_16bit via `unsloth_generic_save()`
  - Loads merged model and applies torchao quantization
  - Saves to `save_directory-torchao` or pushes to Hub

---

### 7. Vision-Specific GGUF Export

**Function: `save_to_gguf_generic`** (Line 2276)
- **Purpose:** GGUF conversion for non-Llama/Mistral models (vision models)
- **Implementation:** Uses `unsloth_zoo.llama_cpp.convert_to_gguf()` for generic model conversion

---

### 8. Method Registration/Patching

**Function: `patch_saving_functions`** (Line 2569)
- **Purpose:** Dynamically attaches all save methods to model instances
- **Called From:** `unsloth/models/llama.py` (lines 2188, 2204, 2902), `unsloth/models/vision.py` (lines 702, 703, 918, 964), `unsloth/chat_templates.py` (line 2309)

**Methods Attached (lines 2658-2676):**
```python
# For non-vision models:
model.push_to_hub_merged = unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged
model.save_pretrained_merged = unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged
model.push_to_hub_gguf = unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf
model.save_pretrained_gguf = unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf
model.save_pretrained_torchao = unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao
model.push_to_hub_ggml = unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub
model.save_pretrained_ggml = unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally

# For vision models:
model.push_to_hub_merged = unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged
model.save_pretrained_merged = unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged
model.push_to_hub_gguf = save_to_gguf_generic
model.save_pretrained_gguf = save_to_gguf_generic
model.save_pretrained_torchao = unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao
```

Also patches `push_to_hub()` to add Unsloth tags (lines 2640-2647)

---

### 9. Implementation Integration Points

**Integration in `unsloth/models/llama.py`:**
- Line 2188: `patch_saving_functions(tokenizer)` - patches tokenizer
- Line 2204: `patch_saving_functions(model)` - patches model after training setup
- Line 2902: `patch_saving_functions(model)` - patches model after inference setup

**Integration in `unsloth/models/vision.py`:**
- Lines 702-703: Patches both model and tokenizer with `vision=True`
- Lines 918, 964: Additional patching for different vision model architectures

---

### Key Features Summary

1. **Three Save Methods:**
   - LoRA only (lightweight, fastest)
   - Merged 16-bit (for GGUF/llama.cpp compatibility)
   - Merged 4-bit (for DPO/inference)

2. **GGUF Export with Multiple Quantizations:**
   - 20+ quantization options (Q2_K through Q8_0, Q4_K variants, etc.)
   - Automatic llama.cpp installation and compilation

3. **Memory Management:**
   - Intelligent GPU/RAM/Disk utilization (lines 562-603)
   - Adaptive serialization based on CPU count
   - Disk-based temporary storage for large models

4. **Hub Integration:**
   - Direct HuggingFace Hub uploads
   - Model metadata tagging
   - Org/user-based repository naming

5. **Format Support:**
   - Standard HF safetensors format
   - GGUF format (llama.cpp)
   - GGML format (LoRA adapters)
   - TorchAO quantized format

6. **Special Handling:**
   - Tokenizer BOS token auto-fix
   - Ollama modelfile generation
   - Support for Kaggle/Colab environments with disk space constraints

### 7. Vision-Specific GGUF Export

**Function: `save_to_gguf_generic`** (Line 2276)
- **Purpose:** GGUF conversion for non-Llama/Mistral models (vision models)
- **Implementation:** Uses `unsloth_zoo.llama_cpp.convert_to_gguf()` for generic model conversion

---

### 8. Method Registration/Patching

**Function: `patch_saving_functions`** (Line 2569)
- **Purpose:** Dynamically attaches all save methods to model instances
- **Called From:** `unsloth/models/llama.py` (lines 2188, 2204, 2902), `unsloth/models/vision.py` (lines 702, 703, 918, 964), `unsloth/chat_templates.py` (line 2309)

**Methods Attached (lines 2658-2676):**
```python
# For non-vision models:
model.push_to_hub_merged = unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged
model.save_pretrained_merged = unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged
model.push_to_hub_gguf = unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf
model.save_pretrained_gguf = unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf
model.save_pretrained_torchao = unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao
model.push_to_hub_ggml = unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub
model.save_pretrained_ggml = unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally

# For vision models:
model.push_to_hub_merged = unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged
model.save_pretrained_merged = unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged
model.push_to_hub_gguf = save_to_gguf_generic
model.save_pretrained_gguf = save_to_gguf_generic
model.save_pretrained_torchao = unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao
```

Also patches `push_to_hub()` to add Unsloth tags (lines 2640-2647)

---

### 9. Implementation Integration Points

**Integration in `unsloth/models/llama.py`:**
- Line 2188: `patch_saving_functions(tokenizer)` - patches tokenizer
- Line 2204: `patch_saving_functions(model)` - patches model after training setup
- Line 2902: `patch_saving_functions(model)` - patches model after inference setup

**Integration in `unsloth/models/vision.py`:**
- Lines 702-703: Patches both model and tokenizer with `vision=True`
- Lines 918, 964: Additional patching for different vision model architectures

---

### Key Features Summary

1. **Three Save Methods:**
   - LoRA only (lightweight, fastest)
   - Merged 16-bit (for GGUF/llama.cpp compatibility)
   - Merged 4-bit (for DPO/inference)

2. **GGUF Export with Multiple Quantizations:**
   - 20+ quantization options (Q2_K through Q8_0, Q4_K variants, etc.)
   - Automatic llama.cpp installation and compilation

3. **Memory Management:**
   - Intelligent GPU/RAM/Disk utilization (lines 562-603)
   - Adaptive serialization based on CPU count
   - Disk-based temporary storage for large models

4. **Hub Integration:**
   - Direct HuggingFace Hub uploads
   - Model metadata tagging
   - Org/user-based repository naming

5. **Format Support:**
   - Standard HF safetensors format
   - GGUF format (llama.cpp)
   - GGML format (LoRA adapters)
   - TorchAO quantized format

6. **Special Handling:**
   - Tokenizer BOS token auto-fix
   - Ollama modelfile generation
   - Support for Kaggle/Colab environments with disk space constraints

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the codebase. Let me compile the report:

## Unsloth Codebase Overview

Based on my thorough exploration of the unsloth repository, here's a comprehensive breakdown of the structure, focusing on model saving/export and key relationships:

---

## 1. **Top-Level Directory Layout**

```
unsloth/
├── __init__.py                    # Main package exports
├── _auto_install.py              # Automatic dependency installation
├── save.py                        # Core model saving/export functionality (105KB)
├── chat_templates.py             # Chat template handling
├── tokenizer_utils.py            # Tokenizer utilities including GGUF support
├── trainer.py                    # Custom trainer classes and configs
├── import_fixes.py               # Pre-import fixes for dependencies
├── dataprep/                     # Data preparation utilities
├── kernels/                      # Optimized CUDA kernels (13 modules)
├── models/                       # Model implementations and loading
├── registry/                     # Model registry system
└── utils/                        # Utility functions (HF Hub integration)
```

---

## 2. **Model Saving/Export Code Location**

### **Primary Saving Module**
**File:** `/unsloth/save.py` (105KB - the heavyweight of model saving)

**Key Exports:**
- `unsloth_save_model()` - Main unified saving function
- `save_to_gguf()` - GGUF format conversion 
- `unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()` - GGUF-specific saver
- `unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf()` - GGUF to HF Hub uploader
- `unsloth_save_pretrained_merged()` - Merged model saver
- `unsloth_push_to_hub_merged()` - Merged model HF uploader
- `unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao()` - TorchAO quantization saver
- `patch_saving_functions()` - Patches model.save_pretrained() methods
- `create_huggingface_repo()` - HF repo creation utility

**Lines 205-761:** `unsloth_save_model()` function - handles:
- LoRA adapter saving
- Merged 16-bit/4-bit model saving
- Push to Hugging Face Hub
- Memory management (max_shard_size, safe_serialization)
- Tokenizer saving with special handling for SentencePiece models

**Lines 952-1320:** `save_to_gguf()` function - handles:
- GGUF format conversion via llama.cpp
- Quantization methods (q4_k_m, q5_k_m, q8_0, etc.)
- llama.cpp installation/compilation
- Support for both SentencePiece and non-SentencePiece models

### **Related Tokenizer Support**
**File:** `/unsloth/tokenizer_utils.py` (41KB)

Key functions:
- `fix_sentencepiece_gguf()` - Handles SentencePiece GGUF conversion
- `fix_tokenizer_bos_token()` - BOS token fixing

---

## 3. **Key Module Relationships**

### **Model Loading Architecture**
**Directory:** `/unsloth/models/`

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `loader.py` (45KB) | Main `FastLanguageModel`, `FastVisionModel`, `FastModel` classes with `.from_pretrained()` |
| `llama.py` (131KB) | Llama model optimizations and implementations (largest single model file) |
| `_utils.py` (68KB) | Shared utilities: quantization, patching, gradient checkpointing |
| `mistral.py` (18KB) | Mistral model specific code |
| `qwen3.py` (18KB) | Qwen3 model implementation |
| `gemma.py`, `gemma2.py` | Gemma variants |
| `cohere.py` | Cohere model support |
| `rl.py`, `rl_replacements.py` | Reinforcement learning specific code |
| `vision.py` (47KB) | Vision model support (multimodal) |
| `mapper.py` (34KB) | Model name mapping (quantized -> base mappings) |
| `loader_utils.py` (6KB) | Helper utilities for model loading |
| `dpo.py` (751B) | DPO trainer patching |

### **Model Registry System**
**Directory:** `/unsloth/registry/`

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `registry.py` | Model registry core: `ModelInfo`, `ModelMeta`, `QuantType` enum, registration functions |
| `__init__.py` | Registry initialization and `search_models()` API |
| `_llama.py`, `_mistral.py`, `_qwen.py`, etc. | Model-specific registration metadata |

Key concepts:
- `QuantType` enum: BNB, UNSLOTH (dynamic 4-bit), GGUF, NONE, BF16
- `ModelInfo` dataclass: tracks org, base_name, version, size, quant_type
- `MODEL_REGISTRY` dict: global registry of all models

### **Training/Inference**
**File:** `/unsloth/trainer.py`

- `UnslothTrainingArguments` - Extended training args
- `UnslothTrainer` - SFTTrainer subclass with custom optimizer for embeddings
- `unsloth_train()` - Training wrapper
- `_patch_trl_trainer()` - TRL backwards compatibility patches

### **Utilities**
**File:** `/unsloth/utils/hf_hub.py`

- `get_model_info()` - HF Hub model info retrieval
- `list_models()` - List models from HF Hub

---

## 4. **Kernels and Optimizations**
**Directory:** `/unsloth/kernels/`

| Component | Files |
|-----------|-------|
| LoRA | `fast_lora.py` - Optimized LoRA forward/backward passes |
| Loss Functions | `cross_entropy_loss.py` - Fused cross entropy |
| Attention | `flex_attention.py` - Flexible attention patterns |
| Embeddings | `rope_embedding.py` - Rotary position embeddings |
| Activations | `swiglu.py`, `geglu.py` - Gated linear unit variants |
| LayerNorms | `layernorm.py`, `rms_layernorm.py` - Optimized normalizations |
| MoE | `moe/` - Mixture of Experts implementation |

---

## 5. **Test Structure**
**Directory:** `/tests/`

| Test Category | Coverage |
|---------------|----------|
| `saving/test_unsloth_save.py` | Main save/load tests with 10+ model variants |
| `saving/language_models/` | Merged model perplexity tests, GRPO models |
| `saving/vision_models/` | Vision model save/merge tests |
| `saving/non_peft/` | Non-PEFT models (Whisper, Mistral) |
| `qlora/` | QLoRA training and merging tests |
| `utils/` | Utility function tests |

---

## 6. **Key File Relationships & Data Flow**

```
User Code
    ↓
FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained() [loader.py]
    ├→ Model name resolution [mapper.py]
    ├→ Registry lookup [registry/]
    ├→ Model-specific loading [llama.py, mistral.py, etc.]
    ├→ Kernel patches applied [kernels/]
    └→ Tokenizer setup [tokenizer_utils.py]
    ↓
Model + Tokenizer
    ↓
unsloth_save_model() [save.py]
    ├→ Merge LoRA if needed [llama.py merge_and_unload()]
    ├→ Save format selection:
    │   ├→ "lora" → PEFT format
    │   ├→ "merged_16bit" → Full FP16 model
    │   ├→ "merged_4bit" → Merged quantized
    │   └→ "torchao" → TorchAO quantized
    ├→ save_to_gguf() [save.py]
    │   └→ llama.cpp integration
    └→ Push to HF Hub [save.py + tokenizer_utils.py]
```

---

## 7. **GGUF-Related Files**

Files containing "gguf" references:
1. `/unsloth/save.py` (main GGUF implementation)
2. `/unsloth/tokenizer_utils.py` (SentencePiece GGUF fixes)
3. `/unsloth/chat_templates.py` (template handling for GGUF)
4. `/unsloth-cli.py` (CLI interface)

**GGUF Implementation Details:**
- Lines 952-1320 in `save.py`: Full GGUF pipeline

**In `/unsloth/registry/registry.py`:**

- `MODEL_REGISTRY`: Global model registry (dict)

---

## 9. **Adapter and Merge Functionality**

**Adapter Handling:**
- LoRA adapters created via `FastLanguageModel.get_peft_model()` (not shown in save.py, part of PEFT)
- `patch_saving_functions()` (line 2569 in save.py) patches `model.save_pretrained()` to handle LoRA correctly
- Default `selected_adapters = ["default"]` when saving LoRA

**Merge Operations:**
- `model.merge_and_unload()` called in `unsloth_save_model()` (line 300)
- Merges LoRA weights into base model weights
- Produces full merged model that doesn't require LoRA at inference time

---

## 10. **Summary of File Count & Structure**

- **Total Python files:** 73 across unsloth/
- **Largest files:** 
  - `llama.py` (131KB) - Llama architecture
  - `save.py` (105KB) - Model saving/export
  - `chat_templates.py` (129KB) - Chat template handling
  - `loader.py` (45KB) - Model loading
  - `rl.py` (45KB) - RL training
- **Model implementations:** 10+ architecture files
- **Kernel optimizations:** 10+ kernel files
- **Test coverage:** Comprehensive tests for all save methods

This structure enables Unsloth to provide unified, optimized model loading and saving with support for multiple quantization formats, training methods, and export targets.

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the codebase. Let me compile the report:

## Unsloth Codebase Overview

Based on my thorough exploration of the unsloth repository, here's a comprehensive breakdown of the structure, focusing on model saving/export and key relationships:

---

## 1. **Top-Level Directory Layout**

```
unsloth/
├── __init__.py                    # Main package exports
├── _auto_install.py              # Automatic dependency installation
├── save.py                        # Core model saving/export functionality (105KB)
├── chat_templates.py             # Chat template handling
├── tokenizer_utils.py            # Tokenizer utilities including GGUF support
├── trainer.py                    # Custom trainer classes and configs
├── import_fixes.py               # Pre-import fixes for dependencies
├── dataprep/                     # Data preparation utilities
├── kernels/                      # Optimized CUDA kernels (13 modules)
├── models/                       # Model implementations and loading
├── registry/                     # Model registry system
└── utils/                        # Utility functions (HF Hub integration)
```

---

## 2. **Model Saving/Export Code Location**

### **Primary Saving Module**
**File:** `/unsloth/save.py` (105KB - the heavyweight of model saving)

**Key Exports:**
- `unsloth_save_model()` - Main unified saving function
- `save_to_gguf()` - GGUF format conversion 
- `unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()` - GGUF-specific saver
- `unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf()` - GGUF to HF Hub uploader
- `unsloth_save_pretrained_merged()` - Merged model saver
- `unsloth_push_to_hub_merged()` - Merged model HF uploader
- `unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao()` - TorchAO quantization saver
- `patch_saving_functions()` - Patches model.save_pretrained() methods
- `create_huggingface_repo()` - HF repo creation utility

**Lines 205-761:** `unsloth_save_model()` function - handles:
- LoRA adapter saving
- Merged 16-bit/4-bit model saving
- Push to Hugging Face Hub
- Memory management (max_shard_size, safe_serialization)
- Tokenizer saving with special handling for SentencePiece models

**Lines 952-1320:** `save_to_gguf()` function - handles:
- GGUF format conversion via llama.cpp
- Quantization methods (q4_k_m, q5_k_m, q8_0, etc.)
- llama.cpp installation/compilation
- Support for both SentencePiece and non-SentencePiece models

### **Related Tokenizer Support**
**File:** `/unsloth/tokenizer_utils.py` (41KB)

Key functions:
- `fix_sentencepiece_gguf()` - Handles SentencePiece GGUF conversion
- `fix_tokenizer_bos_token()` - BOS token fixing

---

## 3. **Key Module Relationships**

### **Model Loading Architecture**
**Directory:** `/unsloth/models/`

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `loader.py` (45KB) | Main `FastLanguageModel`, `FastVisionModel`, `FastModel` classes with `.from_pretrained()` |
| `llama.py` (131KB) | Llama model optimizations and implementations (largest single model file) |
| `_utils.py` (68KB) | Shared utilities: quantization, patching, gradient checkpointing |
| `mistral.py` (18KB) | Mistral model specific code |
| `qwen3.py` (18KB) | Qwen3 model implementation |
| `gemma.py`, `gemma2.py` | Gemma variants |
| `cohere.py` | Cohere model support |
| `rl.py`, `rl_replacements.py` | Reinforcement learning specific code |
| `vision.py` (47KB) | Vision model support (multimodal) |
| `mapper.py` (34KB) | Model name mapping (quantized -> base mappings) |
| `loader_utils.py` (6KB) | Helper utilities for model loading |
| `dpo.py` (751B) | DPO trainer patching |

### **Model Registry System**
**Directory:** `/unsloth/registry/`

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `registry.py` | Model registry core: `ModelInfo`, `ModelMeta`, `QuantType` enum, registration functions |
| `__init__.py` | Registry initialization and `search_models()` API |
| `_llama.py`, `_mistral.py`, `_qwen.py`, etc. | Model-specific registration metadata |

Key concepts:
- `QuantType` enum: BNB, UNSLOTH (dynamic 4-bit), GGUF, NONE, BF16
- `ModelInfo` dataclass: tracks org, base_name, version, size, quant_type
- `MODEL_REGISTRY` dict: global registry of all models

### **Training/Inference**
**File:** `/unsloth/trainer.py`

- `UnslothTrainingArguments` - Extended training args
- `UnslothTrainer` - SFTTrainer subclass with custom optimizer for embeddings
- `unsloth_train()` - Training wrapper
- `_patch_trl_trainer()` - TRL backwards compatibility patches

### **Utilities**
**File:** `/unsloth/utils/hf_hub.py`

- `get_model_info()` - HF Hub model info retrieval
- `list_models()` - List models from HF Hub

---

## 4. **Kernels and Optimizations**
**Directory:** `/unsloth/kernels/`

| Component | Files |
|-----------|-------|
| LoRA | `fast_lora.py` - Optimized LoRA forward/backward passes |
| Loss Functions | `cross_entropy_loss.py` - Fused cross entropy |
| Attention | `flex_attention.py` - Flexible attention patterns |
| Embeddings | `rope_embedding.py` - Rotary position embeddings |
| Activations | `swiglu.py`, `geglu.py` - Gated linear unit variants |
| LayerNorms | `layernorm.py`, `rms_layernorm.py` - Optimized normalizations |
| MoE | `moe/` - Mixture of Experts implementation |

---

## 5. **Test Structure**
**Directory:** `/tests/`

| Test Category | Coverage |
|---------------|----------|
| `saving/test_unsloth_save.py` | Main save/load tests with 10+ model variants |
| `saving/language_models/` | Merged model perplexity tests, GRPO models |
| `saving/vision_models/` | Vision model save/merge tests |
| `saving/non_peft/` | Non-PEFT models (Whisper, Mistral) |
| `qlora/` | QLoRA training and merging tests |
| `utils/` | Utility function tests |

---

## 6. **Key File Relationships & Data Flow**

```
User Code

    └→ Push to HF Hub [save.py + tokenizer_utils.py]
```

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have enough information to create a comprehensive report. Let me compile all the findings:

## Summary of LoRA Structure and Access in Unsloth Codebase

Based on my thorough search of the unsloth codebase, here are the key findings:

### 1. LoRA Layer Structure

**File: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395345315-bq9aoq/unsloth/kernels/utils.py`**

LoRA adapter weights are accessed and extracted through two primary utility functions:

#### Function: `get_lora_parameters()` (Lines 190-232)
- **Location**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395345315-bq9aoq/unsloth/kernels/utils.py:190-232`
- **Returns**: 5-tuple of `(W, quant_state, A, B, scaling)`
  - `W`: Base layer weight (may be quantized)
  - `quant_state`: Quantization state object for 4-bit weights (or None)
  - `A`: LoRA A matrix (input projection) - accessed via `proj.lora_A[adapter].weight` (line 214)
  - `B`: LoRA B matrix (output projection) - accessed via `proj.lora_B[adapter].weight` (line 215)
  - `scaling`: Scaling factor accessed via `proj.scaling[adapter]` (line 230)

**Key Code Snippet:**
```python
adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
adapter = adapter[0]

lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
A = lora_A_linear.weight
B = lora_B_linear.weight

return (W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), A, B, proj.scaling[adapter])
```

#### Function: `get_lora_parameters_bias()` (Lines 235-257)
- **Location**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395345315-bq9aoq/unsloth/kernels/utils.py:235-257`
- **Returns**: 6-tuple including bias term
  - Accesses: `proj.lora_A[adapter].weight`, `proj.lora_B[adapter].weight`, `proj.scaling[adapter]`, and `base_layer.bias`

### 2. LoRA Layer Classes (From PEFT)

**File: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395345315-bq9aoq/unsloth/save.py`**

LoRA layers are identified as instances of:
- **Line 18**: `from peft.tuners.lora import Linear4bit as Peft_Linear4bit`
- **Line 19**: `from peft.tuners.lora import Linear as Peft_Linear`
- **Line 17**: `from bitsandbytes.nn import Linear4bit as Bnb_Linear4bit`

**Usage in Detection (Lines 162-164):**
```python
if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
    W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
```

### 3. LoRA Weight Merging

**File: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395345315-bq9aoq/unsloth/save.py`**

Function: `_merge_lora()` (Lines 159-187)
- **How it works**: 
  - Extracts LoRA weights A and B from layer using `get_lora_parameters_bias()`
  - Dequantizes base weight W if quantized
  - Merges using formula: `W = W + s * A.T @ B.T` (line 176)
  
**Key Code:**
```python
W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
```

### 4. Model Layer Iteration Pattern

**File: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395345315-bq9aoq/unsloth/save.py`**

Layer iteration and LoRA access (Lines 567-602):
```python
for j, layer in enumerate(internal_model.model.layers):
    for item in LLAMA_WEIGHTS:  # "self_attn.q_proj", "mlp.gate_proj", etc.
        proj = eval(f"layer.{item}")
        W, bias = _merge_lora(proj, name)
```

**Identified Weight Names (Lines 67-75):**
```python
LLAMA_WEIGHTS = (
    "self_attn.q_proj", "self_attn.k_proj", "self_attn.v_proj", "self_attn.o_proj",
    "mlp.gate_proj", "mlp.up_proj", "mlp.down_proj",
)
```

### 5. LoRA Config and Metadata

**File: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395345315-bq9aoq/unsloth/models/vision.py`**

Import and usage (Lines 34, 892-894):
```python
from peft import LoraConfig, TaskType, get_peft_model as _get_peft_model

# Creating LoRA config
lora_config = LoraConfig(
    **{ k : v for k, v in local_variables.items() if k in allowed_parameters },
)
```

Configuration parameters accessed include:
- `r`: rank (line 805)
- `target_modules`: target module names (line 806)
- `lora_alpha`: alpha scaling (line 807)
- `lora_dropout`: dropout rate (line 808)
- `bias`: bias handling (line 809)

### 6. LoRA Computation Functions

- **Returns**: 5-tuple of `(W, quant_state, A, B, scaling)`
  - `W`: Base layer weight (may be quantized)
  - `quant_state`: Quantization state object for 4-bit weights (or None)
  - `A`: LoRA A matrix (input projection) - accessed via `proj.lora_A[adapter].weight` (line 214)
  - `B`: LoRA B matrix (output projection) - accessed via `proj.lora_B[adapter].weight` (line 215)
  - `scaling`: Scaling factor accessed via `proj.scaling[adapter]` (line 230)

```python
adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
adapter = adapter[0]

lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
A = lora_A_linear.weight
B = lora_B_linear.weight

return (W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), A, B, proj.scaling[adapter])
```

torch_float16  = torch.float16
torch_bfloat16 = torch.bfloat16

def QUANT_STATE(W): return getattr(W, "quant_state", None)

def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    """
    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.
    """
    # For DPO or disabled adapters
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
    W = base_layer.weight

    # Optionally apply fake quantization to base layer weights for QAT
    weight_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    if weight_fake_quantizer is not None:
        W = weight_fake_quantizer(W)

    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
    pass

    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]

    # Optionally apply fake quantization to lora weights for QAT
    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
    lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    A = lora_A_linear.weight
    B = lora_B_linear.weight
    if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
        A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
    if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
        B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)

    return (
        W,
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
        A,
        B,
        proj.scaling[adapter],
    )
pass


def get_lora_parameters_bias(proj):
    # For DPO or disabled adapters
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
    W = base_layer.weight

    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None, base_layer.bias
    pass

    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]

    return (
        W,
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
        proj.lora_A [adapter].weight,
        proj.lora_B [adapter].weight,
        proj.scaling[adapter],
        base_layer.bias,
    )
pass

    """

    Otherwise, just return the input activations as is.
    Weights are fake quantized separately in `get_lora_parameters`.
